attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches between 200-500m depending on leadout delivery. When isolated or lacking train support, prone to launching too early (250m+) and fading in final 100m.
team_tactics: Heavily dependent on organized leadout trains (Quick-Step/UAE structures). Without perfect delivery, shows weak positional instincts—often caught wrong side of leadouts, mid-pack, or behind crashes within final 3km. Team sometimes parasitically uses rival trains when own dissolves.
terrain_preference: Pure flat stages with long, straight finishes. Exposed on any uphill finishes (fades badly when launching at 500m on drags) and dropped on even moderate climbs 6-7km from summit. Technical descents surprisingly strong for sprinter category.
vulnerability: Climbing limitations exploited on rolling sprint stages (dropped by 10km tempo). Crashes frequently in technical finales and during clothing changes. Fades badly in final 50-100m when launching from front or without leadout. Poor self-positioning without train structure.
key_patterns: Fades final 50-100m when leading sprint from 200-250m out, indicating insufficient top-end speed finish (6+ instances)
Dropped on moderate climbs 6-7km before summit on rolling sprint stages when pace controlled (4 instances)
Crashes or caught behind crashes in final 3km with notable frequency during technical approaches (5+ instances)
Strongest when leadout trains fracture into chaos; navigates messy sprints better than structured ones (4 Giro 2017 wins)
Contesting intermediate sprints for points jersey depletes finishing speed in stage finales (3 documented instances)
Positional errors: wrong side of leadout, mid-pack reliance on raw speed, choosing wrong wheels (6+ instances)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 shows continued decline from 2017-2018 peak—launching early and fading, crashes during service, losing leads from 250m. No wins since 2019 gift DQ upgrade suggests velocity deficit persists.
